text: Let's {{c1::touch base}} tomorrow to discuss the updates.
explanation: touch base is a common business idiom meaning to briefly contact or meet with someone to talk about progress.
usage:
Pattern: touch base with [someone] [time]
  • I need to touch base with my manager before the client call.
  • Let's touch base next week.
spanish: Póngamonos en contacto mañana para discutir las actualizaciones.

text: We need to {{c1::push back}} the meeting to 3 PM.
explanation: push back means to postpone or reschedule an event to a later time or date.
usage:
Pattern: push back [event] to [time/date]
  • Can we push back the project deadline by two days?
  • They had to push back the launch due to technical issues.
spanish: Necesitamos posponer la reunión para las 3 PM.

text: I'll {{c1::follow up with}} you via email once I get the data.
explanation: follow up with means to contact someone again to provide further information, verify progress, or request updates.
usage:
Pattern: follow up with [someone] on/via [method]
  • The doctor will follow up with you next month.
  • I will follow up with the client on the contract details tomorrow.
spanish: Le daré seguimiento por correo electrónico una vez que obtenga los datos.

text: During the planning sync, the director shared: "Our {{c1::financial forecast}} projects new {{c2::revenue streams}} from our enterprise API licensing model."
explanation: Financial forecast is a projection of future financial performance. **Revenue streams** refer to different sources of income for a business.
usage:
Pattern: financial forecast projects [metric] / identify new revenue streams
  • The financial forecast projects a 15% increase in sales.
  • We are exploring new revenue streams to diversify our income.
spanish: Durante la sincronización de planificación, el director compartió: "Nuestro pronóstico financiero proyecta nuevas fuentes de ingresos a partir de nuestro modelo de licencia de API empresarial".

text: Could you please take notes during the presentation and share them with the team {{c1::afterwards}}?
explanation: 'Afterwards' is an adverb meaning at a later or subsequent time; after something else has occurred.
usage:
Pattern: afterwards / after the meeting
  • We had a brief discussion afterwards.
  • She completed the task and left immediately afterwards.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar notas durante la presentación y compartirlas con el equipo después/posteriormente?

text: Let's go around the table and have everyone briefly introduce themselves and their {{c1::area of expertise}}.
explanation: An 'area of expertise' is the specific field, subject, or skill set in which a person is highly knowledgeable or skilled.
usage:
Pattern: area of expertise / domain knowledge
  • Database performance tuning is my main area of expertise.
  • We hired consultants with expertise in cloud migration.
spanish: Hagamos una ronda en la mesa para que todos se presenten brevemente y mencionen su área de especialización.

text: I'd like to share my screen to show you the latest mockup designs for the {{c1::landing page}}.
explanation: A 'landing page' is the specific web page that a user arrives at after clicking a link or advertisement, designed to convert visitors.
usage:
Pattern: landing page / homepage
  • We optimized the landing page to increase sign-ups.
  • The marketing campaign directs traffic to a custom landing page.
spanish: Me gustaría compartir mi pantalla para mostrarles los últimos diseños de maqueta para la página de destino (inicio).

text: Let's reserve the last ten minutes of the meeting for a quick Q&A session to address any {{c1::remaining questions}}.
explanation: A 'Q&A session' (Question and Answer) is a dedicated time during a meeting or presentation for the audience to ask questions of the speaker.
usage:
Pattern: Q&A session / questions and answers
  • The webinar ended with a lively Q&A session.
  • We will take questions during the Q&A session at the end.
spanish: Reservemos los últimos diez minutos de la reunión para una sesión rápida de preguntas y respuestas para abordar cualquier pregunta restante.

text: I agree with your suggestion, let's incorporate that feedback into the next iteration of the {{c1::proposal}}.
explanation: A 'proposal' is a formal written document or offer presented to others for consideration, typically detailing a plan, budget, or contract.
usage:
Pattern: project proposal / business proposal
  • We submitted our proposal to the client yesterday.
  • The board will review the proposal at the next meeting.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su sugerencia, incorporemos esa retroalimentación en la próxima iteración de la propuesta.

text: Before we begin, let's quickly review the {{c1::agenda items}} to make sure we allocate our time properly.
explanation: Agenda items are the individual topics or tasks scheduled to be discussed during a meeting.
usage:
Pattern: agenda items / meeting agenda
  • We have five agenda items to cover today.
  • Please add this topic to the agenda items for next week.
spanish: Antes de comenzar, revisemos rápidamente los puntos de la agenda para asegurarnos de asignar nuestro tiempo adecuadamente.

text: Could you please take minutes during the meeting and email them to the attendees {{c1::by tomorrow morning}}?
explanation: 'By tomorrow morning' is a common business deadline, requesting that a task be completed and sent before the start of the next workday.
usage:
Pattern: by tomorrow morning / first thing tomorrow
  • I'll have the code review ready by tomorrow morning.
  • Please send the contract to the client by tomorrow morning.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar la minuta durante la reunión y enviarla por correo electrónico a los asistentes para mañana por la mañana?

text: Let's introduce our new team member, Colin, who will be taking over the database migration {{c1::project}}.
explanation: A 'project' is an individual or collaborative enterprise that is carefully planned and designed to achieve a particular aim.
usage:
Pattern: project manager / project milestones
  • I am managing the new API development project.
  • The database migration project was completed on schedule.
spanish: Presentemos a nuestro nuevo miembro del equipo, Colin, quien se hará cargo del proyecto de migración de la base de datos.

text: I'd like to present the user flow diagram to show how the customer registers on our {{c1::platform}}.
explanation: A 'platform' is a major software environment, website, or infrastructure where services are hosted and executed.
usage:
Pattern: software platform / cloud platform
  • Our platform supports over 10,000 concurrent connections.
  • We deployed our application on a secure hosting platform.
spanish: Me gustaría presentar el diagrama de flujo de usuario para mostrar cómo el cliente se registra en nuestra plataforma.

text: We will allocate the last fifteen minutes of the meeting for an open discussion to address any {{c1::concerns}}.
explanation: A 'concern' is a matter of interest, anxiety, or worry raised by team members or clients regarding a plan or change.
usage:
Pattern: address concerns / voice concerns
  • The director met with staff to address their concerns about the restructuring.
  • If you have any safety concerns, please voice them immediately.
spanish: Asignaremos los últimos quince minutos de la reunión para una discusión abierta para abordar cualquier preocupación.

text: I agree with your proposal; let's schedule a follow-up call to plan the implementation {{c1::steps}}.
explanation: 'Steps' refer to the phases or individual tasks required to carry out a proposal or plan.
usage:
Pattern: implementation steps / action steps
  • The document outlines the implementation steps for the database migration.
  • We followed the configuration steps exactly.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su propuesta; programemos una llamada de seguimiento para planificar los pasos de implementación.

text: Please make sure you have the report ready before the meeting starts so we can reference the {{c1::metrics}}.
explanation: 'Metrics' are measures of quantitative assessment commonly used for assessing, comparing, and tracking performance (e.g. latency, conversions).
usage:
Pattern: performance metrics / tracking metrics
  • Our primary performance metric is API response time.
  • Review the marketing metrics before launching the campaign.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de tener el informe listo antes de que comience la reunión para que podamos hacer referencia a las métricas.

text: If there are no objections, let's proceed with the plan and assign tasks to the respective team {{c1::members}}.
explanation: A 'member' is an individual belonging to a group or team.
usage:
Pattern: team members / staff members
  • All team members must attend the daily stand-up meeting.
  • We welcomed three new members to our division.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, procedamos con el plan y asignemos tareas a los respectivos miembros del equipo.

text: I'd like to share a quick update on our customer acquisition costs for this past {{c1::quarter}}.
explanation: A 'quarter' (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) is a three-month period in a financial calendar, used to report business earnings and metrics.
usage:
Pattern: fiscal quarter / third quarter / Q3
  • Our revenue increased by 10% in the last quarter.
  • We are planning our goals for the upcoming quarter.
spanish: Me gustaría compartir una actualización rápida sobre nuestros costos de adquisición de clientes para este último trimestre.

text: Let's keep the discussion focused on the project milestones and avoid discussing future product {{c1::features}}.
explanation: A 'feature' is a distinctive attribute or aspect of a software application (e.g. user authentication, dark mode).
usage:
Pattern: product features / new features
  • The developer is coding the new search feature.
  • Users requested a data export feature.
spanish: Mantengamos la discusión enfocada en los hitos del proyecto y evitemos discutir futuras características del producto.

text: Thank you all for your feedback today; we will use these insights to refine the project design before our next {{c1::sync}}.
explanation: A 'sync' (synchronization) is business jargon for a regular meeting scheduled to align team members on project updates.
usage:
Pattern: weekly sync / schedule a sync
  • Let's schedule a quick sync call tomorrow morning.
  • The weekly sync is scheduled for Tuesdays at 10:00 AM.
spanish: Gracias a todos por sus comentarios de hoy; utilizaremos estos conocimientos para refinar el diseño del proyecto antes de nuestra próxima reunión de sincronización.
